verses.latin_vulgate: Aderant autem quidam ipso in tempore, nuntiantes illi de Galilæis, quorum sanguinem Pilatus miscuit cum sacrificiis eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρῆσαν δέ τινες ἐν αὐτῷ τῷ καιρῷ ἀπαγγέλλοντες αὐτῷ περὶ τῶν γαλιλαίων ὧν τὸ αἷμα πιλᾶτος ἔμιξεν μετὰ τῶν θυσιῶν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: There were present at that season some that told him of the Galilaeans, whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens dixit illis: Putatis quod hi Galilæi præ omnibus Galilæis peccatores fuerint, quia talia passi sunt?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς δοκεῖτε ὅτι οἱ γαλιλαῖοι οὗτοι ἁμαρτωλοὶ παρὰ πάντας τοὺς γαλιλαίους ἐγένοντο ὅτι τοιαῦτα πεπόνθασιν
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering said unto them, Suppose ye that these Galilaeans were sinners above all the Galilaeans, because they suffered such things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non, dico vobis: sed nisi pœnitentiam habueritis, omnes similiter peribitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχί λέγω ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ ἐὰν μὴ μετανοῆτε πάντες ὡσαύτως ἀπολεῖσθε
verses.king_james: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut illi decem et octo, supra quos cecidit turris in Siloë, et occidit eos: putatis quia et ipsi debitores fuerint præter omnes homines habitantes in Jerusalem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ ἐκεῖνοι οἱ δεκα καὶ οκτὼ, ἐφ᾽ οὓς ἔπεσεν ὁ πύργος ἐν τῷ σιλωὰμ καὶ ἀπέκτεινεν αὐτούς δοκεῖτε ὅτι οὐτοὶ ὀφειλέται ἐγένοντο παρὰ πάντας ἀνθρώπους τοὺς κατοικοῦντας ἐν ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non, dico vobis: sed si pœnitentiam non egeritis, omnes similiter peribitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχί λέγω ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ ἐὰν μὴ μετανοῆτε πάντες ὁμοίως ἀπολεῖσθε
verses.king_james: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem et hanc similitudinem: Arborem fici habebat quidam plantatam in vinea sua, et venit quærens fructum in illa, et non invenit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ ταύτην τὴν παραβολήν συκῆν εἶχέν τις ἐν τῷ ἀμπελῶνι αὐτοῦ πεφυτευμένην καὶ ἦλθεν καρπὸν ζητῶν ἐν αὐτῇ καὶ οὐχ εὗρεν
verses.king_james: He spake also this parable; A certain *man* had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ad cultorem vineæ: Ecce anni tres sunt ex quo venio quærens fructum in ficulnea hac, et non invenio: succide ergo illam: ut quid etiam terram occupat?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς τὸν ἀμπελουργόν ἰδού, τρία ἔτη ἔρχομαι ζητῶν καρπὸν ἐν τῇ συκῇ ταύτῃ καὶ οὐχ εὑρίσκω ἔκκοψον αὐτήν ἱνατί καὶ τὴν γῆν καταργεῖ
verses.king_james: Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondens, dicit illi: Domine dimitte illam et hoc anno, usque dum fodiam circa illam, et mittam stercora,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς λέγει αὐτῷ κύριε ἄφες αὐτὴν καὶ τοῦτο τὸ ἔτος ἕως ὅτου σκάψω περὶ αὐτὴν καὶ βάλω κόπριαν,
verses.king_james: And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung *it*:

verses.latin_vulgate: et siquidem fecerit fructum: sin autem, in futurum succides eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἂν μὲν ποιήσῃ καρπὸν εἰ δὲ μήγε, εἰς τὸ μέλλον ἐκκόψεις αὐτήν
verses.king_james: And if it bear fruit, *well*: and if not, *then* after that thou shalt cut it down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem docens in synagoga eorum sabbatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ διδάσκων ἐν μιᾷ τῶν συναγωγῶν ἐν τοῖς σάββασιν
verses.king_james: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce mulier, quæ habebat spiritum infirmitatis annis decem et octo: et erat inclinata, nec omnino poterat sursum respicere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, γυνὴ ἦν πνεῦμα ἔχουσα ἀσθενείας ἔτη δέκα καὶ ὀκτὼ, καὶ ἦν συγκύπτουσα καὶ μὴ δυναμένη ἀνακύψαι εἰς τὸ παντελές
verses.king_james: And, behold, there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and was bowed together, and could in no wise lift up *herself*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam cum videret Jesus, vocavit eam ad se, et ait illi: Mulier, dimissa es ab infirmitate tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ αὐτὴν ὁ ἰησοῦς προσεφώνησεν καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ γύναι ἀπολέλυσαι τῆς ἀσθενείας σου
verses.king_james: And when Jesus saw her, he called *her to him*, and said unto her, Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et imposuit illi manus, et confestim erecta est, et glorificabat Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπέθηκεν αὐτῇ τὰς χεῖρας καὶ παραχρῆμα ἀνωρθώθη καὶ ἐδόξαζεν τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: And he laid *his* hands on her: and immediately she was made straight, and glorified God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem archisynagogus, indignans quia sabbato curasset Jesus, dicebat turbæ: Sex dies sunt in quibus oportet operari: in his ergo venite, et curamini, et non in die sabbati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἀρχισυνάγωγος ἀγανακτῶν ὅτι τῷ σαββάτῳ ἐθεράπευσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἔλεγεν τῷ ὄχλῳ ἓξ ἡμέραι εἰσὶν ἐν αἷς δεῖ ἐργάζεσθαι ἐν ταύταις οὖν ἐρχόμενοι θεραπεύεσθε καὶ μὴ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ σαββάτου
verses.king_james: And the ruler of the synagogue answered with indignation, because that Jesus had healed on the sabbath day, and said unto the people, There are six days in which men ought to work: in them therefore come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem ad illum Dominus, dixit: Hypocritæ, unusquisque vestrum sabbato non solvit bovem suum, aut asinum a præsepio, et ducit adaquare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη οὖν αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος καὶ εἶπεν ὑποκριτά ἕκαστος ὑμῶν τῷ σαββάτῳ οὐ λύει τὸν βοῦν αὐτοῦ ἢ τὸν ὄνον ἀπὸ τῆς φάτνης καὶ ἀπαγαγὼν ποτίζει
verses.king_james: The Lord then answered him, and said, *Thou* hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or *his* ass from the stall, and lead *him* away to watering?

verses.latin_vulgate: Hanc autem filiam Abrahæ, quam alligavit Satanas, ecce decem et octo annis, non oportuit solvi a vinculo isto die sabbati?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταύτην δὲ θυγατέρα ἀβραὰμ οὖσαν ἣν ἔδησεν ὁ σατανᾶς ἰδού, δέκα καὶ ὀκτὼ ἔτη οὐκ ἔδει λυθῆναι ἀπὸ τοῦ δεσμοῦ τούτου τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ σαββάτου
verses.king_james: And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum hæc diceret, erubescant omnes adversarii ejus: et omnis populus gaudebat in universis, quæ gloriosæ fiebant ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα λέγοντος αὐτοῦ κατῃσχύνοντο πάντες οἱ ἀντικείμενοι αὐτῷ καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ἔχαιρεν ἐπὶ πᾶσιν τοῖς ἐνδόξοις τοῖς γινομένοις ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when he had said these things, all his adversaries were ashamed: and all the people rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat ergo: Cui simile est regnum Dei, et cui simile æstimabo illud?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ, τίνι ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τίνι ὁμοιώσω αὐτήν
verses.king_james: Then said he, Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I resemble it?

verses.latin_vulgate: Simile est grano sinapis, quod acceptum homo misit in hortum suum, et crevit, et factum est in arborem magnam: et volucres cæli requieverunt in ramis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοία ἐστὶν κόκκῳ σινάπεως ὃν λαβὼν ἄνθρωπος ἔβαλεν εἰς κῆπον ἑαυτοῦ καὶ ηὔξησεν καὶ ἐγένετο εἰς δένδρον μέγα, καὶ τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ κατεσκήνωσεν ἐν τοῖς κλάδοις αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his garden; and it grew, and waxed a great tree; and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum dixit: Cui simile æstimabo regnum Dei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν εἶπεν τίνι ὁμοιώσω τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And again he said, Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Simile est fermento, quod acceptum mulier abscondit in farinæ sata tria, donec fermentaretur totum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοία ἐστὶν ζύμῃ ἣν λαβοῦσα γυνὴ ἐνέκρυψεν εἰς ἀλεύρου σάτα τρία ἕως οὗ ἐζυμώθη ὅλον
verses.king_james: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ibat per civitates et castella, docens, et iter faciens in Jerusalem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διεπορεύετο κατὰ πόλεις καὶ κώμας διδάσκων καὶ πορείαν ποιούμενος εἰς ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: And he went through the cities and villages, teaching, and journeying toward Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem illi quidam: Domine, si pauci sunt, qui salvantur? Ipse autem dixit ad illos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δέ τις αὐτῷ κύριε εἰ ὀλίγοι οἱ σῳζόμενοι ὁ δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: Then said one unto him, Lord, are there few that be saved? And he said unto them,

verses.latin_vulgate: Contendite intrare per angustam portam: quia multi, dico vobis, quærent intrare, et non poterunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγωνίζεσθε εἰσελθεῖν διὰ τῆς στενῆς πύλης ὅτι πολλοί λέγω ὑμῖν ζητήσουσιν εἰσελθεῖν καὶ οὐκ ἰσχύσουσιν
verses.king_james: Strive to enter in at the strait gate: for many, I say unto you, will seek to enter in, and shall not be able.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem intraverit paterfamilias, et clauserit ostium, incipietis foris stare, et pulsare ostium, dicentes: Domine, aperi nobis: et respondens dicet vobis: Nescio vos unde sitis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφ᾽ οὗ ἂν ἐγερθῇ ὁ οἰκοδεσπότης καὶ ἀποκλείσῃ τὴν θύραν καὶ ἄρξησθε ἔξω ἑστάναι καὶ κρούειν τὴν θύραν λέγοντες κύριε κύριε, ἄνοιξον ἡμῖν καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ἐρεῖ ὑμῖν οὐκ οἶδα ὑμᾶς πόθεν ἐστέ
verses.king_james: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are:

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc incipietis dicere: Manducavimus coram te, et bibimus, et in plateis nostris docuisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἄρξεσθε λέγειν ἐφάγομεν ἐνώπιόν σου καὶ ἐπίομεν καὶ ἐν ταῖς πλατείαις ἡμῶν ἐδίδαξας
verses.king_james: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicet vobis: Nescio vos unde sitis: discedite a me omnes operarii iniquitatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐρεῖ λέγω ὑμῖν οὐκ οἶδα ὑμᾶς πόθεν ἐστέ ἀπόστητε ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ πάντες οἱ ἐργάται τῆς ἀδικίας
verses.king_james: But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all *ye* workers of iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ibi erit fletus et stridor dentium: cum videritis Abraham, et Isaac, et Jacob, et omnes prophetas in regno Dei, vos autem expelli foras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων ὅταν ὄψησθε ἀβραὰμ καὶ ἰσαὰκ καὶ ἰακὼβ καὶ πάντας τοὺς προφήτας ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ ὑμᾶς δὲ ἐκβαλλομένους ἔξω
verses.king_james: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and you *yourselves* thrust out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venient ab oriente, et occidente, et aquilone, et austro, et accumbent in regno Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἥξουσιν ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν καὶ δυσμῶν καὶ ἀπὸ βοῤῥᾶ καὶ νότου καὶ ἀνακλιθήσονται ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And they shall come from the east, and *from* the west, and from the north, and *from* the south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce sunt novissimi qui erunt primi, et sunt primi qui erunt novissimi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, εἰσὶν ἔσχατοι οἳ ἔσονται πρῶτοι καὶ εἰσὶν πρῶτοι οἳ ἔσονται ἔσχατοι
verses.king_james: And, behold, there are last which shall be first, and there are first which shall be last.

verses.latin_vulgate: In ipsa die accesserunt quidam pharisæorum, dicentes illi: Exi, et vade hinc: quia Herodes vult te occidere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν αὐτῇ τῇ ἡμέρα προσῆλθόν τινες φαρισαῖοι λέγοντες αὐτῷ ἕξελθε καὶ πορεύου ἐντεῦθεν ὅτι ἡρῴδης θέλει σε ἀποκτεῖναι
verses.king_james: The same day there came certain of the Pharisees, saying unto him, Get thee out, and depart hence: for Herod will kill thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Ite, et dicite vulpi illi: Ecce ejicio dæmonia, et sanitates perficio hodie, et cras, et tertia die consummor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πορευθέντες εἴπατε τῇ ἀλώπεκι ταύτῃ ἰδού, ἐκβάλλω δαιμόνια καὶ ἰάσεις ἐπιτελῶ σήμερον καὶ αὔριον καὶ τῇ τρίτῃ τελειοῦμαι
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third *day* I shall be perfected.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen oportet me hodie et cras et sequenti die ambulare: quia non capit prophetam perire extra Jerusalem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν δεῖ με σήμερον καὶ αὔριον καὶ τῇ ἐχομένῃ πορεύεσθαι ὅτι οὐκ ἐνδέχεται προφήτην ἀπολέσθαι ἔξω ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the *day* following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, quæ occidis prophetas, et lapidas eos qui mittuntur ad te, quoties volui congregare filios tuos quemadmodum avis nidum suum sub pennis, et noluisti?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰερουσαλὴμ ἰερουσαλήμ ἡ ἀποκτείνουσα τοὺς προφήτας καὶ λιθοβολοῦσα τοὺς ἀπεσταλμένους πρὸς αὐτήν ποσάκις ἠθέλησα ἐπισυνάξαι τὰ τέκνα σου ὃν τρόπον ὄρνις τὴν ἑαυτῆς νοσσιὰν ὑπὸ τὰς πτέρυγας καὶ οὐκ ἠθελήσατε
verses.king_james: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen *doth gather* her brood under *her* wings, and ye would not!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce relinquetur vobis domus vestra deserta. Dico autem vobis, quia non videbitis me donec veniat cum dicetis: Benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού, ἀφίεται ὑμῖν ὁ οἶκος ὑμῶν ἔρημος ἀμὴν δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ με ἴδητέ ἕως ἄν ἥξῃ, ὅτε εἴπητε εὐλογημένος ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου
verses.king_james: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until *the time* come when ye shall say, Blessed *is* he that cometh in the name of the Lord.
